creator: chris+freebsd-PR@revolt.com
text:
The Pentium D EMT64 on a SuperMicro board will not boot with ACPI enabled on 6.2-RELEASE (GENERIC).

While this isn't a huge deal (simply boot w/o ACPI from loader), it does
mean that some manual intervention is required to both install and run
FreeBSD on this machine. Verbose dmesg of hung start attached:


| / - \ | / - SMAP type=01 base=0000000000000000 len=000000000009c800
SMAP type=02 base=000000000009c800 len=0000000000003800
SMAP type=02 base=00000000000e4000 len=000000000001c000
SMAP type=01 base=0000000000100000 len=00000000dfde0000
SMAP type=03 base=00000000dfee0000 len=0000000000009000
SMAP type=04 base=00000000dfee9000 len=0000000000017000
SMAP type=02 base=00000000dff00000 len=0000000000100000
SMAP type=02 base=00000000fec00000 len=0000000000010000
SMAP type=02 base=00000000fee00000 len=0000000000001000
SMAP type=02 base=00000000ff000000 len=0000000001000000
SMAP type=01 base=0000000100000000 len=0000000020000000
